title: Markdown History and Lineage
description: Markdown is a lightweight markup language created by John Gruber in 2004 (with Aaron Swartz as a sounding board) to create formatted text in plain-text editors that can optionally be converted to structurally valid HTML/XHTML. It was invented to prioritize readability over 'heavier' markup languages like RTF or HTML, allowing text to be readable as-is without looking like it has been marked up with tags. Its lineage stems from pre-existing plain text conventions in email and Usenet posts, as well as earlier lightweight languages such as setext (1992), Textile (2002), and reStructuredText (2002).
